mnemonics: FIFO (First In, First Out) - Like a line at a food counter: the first person to join is the first to be served.
keyPoints: A queue uses two pointers: front (for dequeue) and rear (for enqueue)
Array implementation requires circular handling to avoid false 'overflow'
Operations: enqueue (add to rear), dequeue (remove from front), peek (view front)
commonMistakes: Not checking for queue underflow before dequeue
Not properly handling the circular nature of the array, leading to unused spaces
Incorrectly updating front and rear pointers, especially when queue becomes empty
